description: Figure 2 presents the microstructural characterization of tempered low alloy ultrahigh strength steels. Panel (a) shows a scanning electron microscopy (SEM) image of the 1.3Al steel revealing a tempered martensitic structure characterized by lath martensite morphology, with an inset displaying higher magnification transmission electron microscopy (TEM) details of the substructure at 0.2 μm scale. Panel (b) displays X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns comparing 0Al and 1.3Al steels, identifying body-centered cubic (bcc) martensite (α) peaks including (110), (200), (211), and (220), and face-centered cubic (fcc) retained austenite (γ) peaks including (111) and (200). The XRD analysis indicates retained austenite contents of 1.0% for 0Al steel and 1.8% for 1.3Al steel. Dislocation densities calculated using the Williamson-Hall method from the broadening of martensitic peaks are ~2.01×10^15 m^-2 for 0Al steel and ~2.20×10^15 m^-2 for 1.3Al steel, showing a slight increase upon Al addition. Both steels were subjected to solution treatment at 970°C for 1 h followed by water quenching and tempering at 180°C for 2 h.
